description:
Your team uses plain-text files under /home/user/system_config/ to keep
    user-level copies of time-zone and locale settings (no root access needed).
    Two files are already present:

      1. /home/user/system_config/timezone.conf     (current content: “TIMEZONE=UTC”)
      2. /home/user/system_config/locale.conf       (current content: “LANG=C”)

    A configuration-management daemon scans these files once a minute and
    looks for a companion change-log stored at
    /home/user/config-changes/time_locale.log.  The daemon refuses to
    propagate your edits unless the log exists *and* its exact format matches
    strict rules.

    Task
    ----
    1. Change the time-zone setting to America/New_York.
    2. Change the locale setting to en_US.UTF-8.
    3. Create (or overwrite) the log file
       /home/user/config-changes/time_locale.log that summarises the change.

    Required final state
    --------------------
    • /home/user/system_config/timezone.conf must contain exactly (including
      capitalisation and the trailing newline):
          TIMEZONE=America/New_York\n

    • /home/user/system_config/locale.conf must contain exactly:
          LANG=en_US.UTF-8\n

    • /home/user/config-changes/time_locale.log must exist and contain *only*
      the two lines below, in this order, terminated by a single newline at
      the very end of the file (no spaces before or after arrows, no extra
      blank lines):

          TIMEZONE:UTC->America/New_York
          LANG:C->en_US.UTF-8

    Directory notes
    ---------------
    • If /home/user/config-changes/ does not yet exist, create it with default
      user permissions (do **not** use sudo).
    • Do not rename or delete any existing files.
    • No additional text, comments or timestamps are allowed in the log.

    When you are done, the daemon should be able to verify the updates by
    comparing the file contents and the log.  You only need 2–3 shell
    commands to finish the job.
truth:
Initial files (already present before the task starts):
    -------------------------------------------------------
    1. Path: /home/user/system_config/timezone.conf
       Content (single line, newline terminated):
           TIMEZONE=UTC\n
    2. Path: /home/user/system_config/locale.conf
       Content (single line, newline terminated):
           LANG=C\n

    Expected final state after agent completes the task:
    ----------------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/system_config/timezone.conf
       Content:
           TIMEZONE=America/New_York\n
    2. /home/user/system_config/locale.conf
       Content:
           LANG=en_US.UTF-8\n
    3. /home/user/config-changes/time_locale.log
       Content (exactly):
           TIMEZONE:UTC->America/New_York\n
           LANG:C->en_US.UTF-8\n
name: task_000000_f4469eea
